narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
narratives.reasoning: The text implies that the Western media and possibly the West itself are responsible for prolonging the war, shifting the blame away from Russia's actions as the invader.

narratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narratives.reasoning: The text directly attacks and discredits the West, specifically Americans, using highly derogatory language, indicating a strong negative sentiment towards Western countries and their perceived actions or policies.

narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat
narratives.reasoning: This quote portrays Russia as being unfairly perceived as a threat by the West, suggesting that Russia is a victim of Western misconceptions and fear-mongering.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death
narratives.reasoning: The text expresses distrust towards Western media, suggesting that it spreads misinformation or propaganda about the war in Ukraine, specifically about Russia's performance in the conflict.

narratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war? …lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: The text amplifies fears about the war escalating into a nuclear conflict, discussing the possibility of nuclear weapons being used and the devastating consequences of such an event.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the West, through its media, is responsible for prolonging the war and causing more death, implying that they are the aggressors or at least contributing to the escalation of the conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by shifting the blame from Russia to the West, implying that the West has historically exaggerated or fabricated threats from Russia and other countries, but it does not fit neatly into the provided subnarrative definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the West, through its media, is misrepresenting the situation in Ukraine, implying an overreaction that prolongs the conflict and increases casualties, aligning with the narrative that the West is overreacting.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This question implies that the West's actions are driven by self-interest rather than a genuine concern for Ukraine, suggesting that the West's primary motivation is not the well-being of Ukraine but its own interests.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement reflects a strong anti-Western sentiment, specifically targeting Americans with derogatory language, indicating a broader discrediting of the West beyond the specific subnarratives listed, as it does not fit neatly into the other defined subnarratives but clearly supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: The West is russophobic
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the West has a long-standing tendency to unfairly portray Russia as a threat, which aligns with the subnarrative that the West is russophobic.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Russia actions in Ukraine are only self-defence
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the Western media's portrayal of the war is inaccurate and that Russia's actions are being misrepresented, which could be seen as justifying Russia's actions as self-defence.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I am sure Scott will have a safe place he can go .. he talks the most sense ….pity he cannot all but talk about it….most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind of Russia China`Britain (lots of europe) amerika canada australia NZ
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote expresses a sense of desperation and hopelessness, implying that the West is responsible for the escalation of the conflict and that Russia is not the aggressor, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit into the other specific subnarrative definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly supports the subnarrative that Western media is an instrument of propaganda by suggesting that it is intentionally misrepresenting the situation in Ukraine to prolong the conflict.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that Western media is not only biased but also withholding important information, which supports the broader narrative of distrust towards media without fitting neatly into the other specific subnarratives provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war? ...lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: The text evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, suggesting that the situation is dire enough that such an extreme outcome is not only possible but perhaps even preferable to the current state of affairs, thus fitting the definition of this subnarrative.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
subnarratives.reasoning: The title implies that the situation in Ukraine is serious and that the nuclear threat is a sign of desperation, which suggests that continuing the war could lead to a larger, more catastrophic conflict, such as WWIII, thus warning against the escalation of the conflict.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: time to stop caring about the people that do not care..they are the enemy of one sort or the other.. time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name
subnarratives.reasoning: This part of the text supports the parent narrative by expressing frustration and hopelessness about the situation, implying that the actions of Western countries and their politicians are leading to devastating consequences, and that the general population is either complicit or indifferent, thus advancing a broader fear-related narrative about the war that doesn't fit neatly into the other defined subnarratives.
